# Type-fix brief — resolve sql-orm-client type-d failures + push

**Branch:** `tml-2526-facades-must-re-export-everything-users-import-in-their-app`
**Worktree:** `/Users/wmadden/Projects/prisma/prisma-next-ws/worktrees/tml-2526-facades-must-re-export-everything-users-import-in-their-app`
**Current state:** rebased onto `origin/main` (`cda126a566`) + force-pushed to `origin` at `394c62cfe`. CI is red on Type Check; you'll turn it green and push the fix.

## Investigation hand-off (already done — do NOT redo)

The prior diagnostic dispatch (`301157c7`) produced this finding set; both files are read-only inputs for you:

- `wip/heartbeats/typecheck-orm-client.log` — ground truth typecheck log (16 errors, 4 files).
- Hand-off summary in the prior subagent's stand-down report (also recorded as `1db94e76f`'s file-by-file table in `wip/heartbeats/typecheck-investigation.txt`).

Key conclusions from that investigation — accept them as the starting position unless your work directly contradicts them:

1. **Attribution: 100% our-branch regression**, not main drift. `origin/main` made zero commits to `sql-orm-client/src`, `relational-core`, `contract-ts`, or this package's fixtures since the merge-base. None of the 4 failing test files were touched on either branch.
2. **16 errors decompose into 2 root causes:**
   - **13 errors (one root cause):** `codec-async.types.test-d.ts` (5) + `value-object-inputs.test-d.ts` (2) + `simplify-deep.test-d.ts` (6) all fail in the same shape — `AddressShape | null` (and field-shape) expectations collapse to `never` against the actual `DefaultModelRow` / `CreateInput`. Hypothesis: the regenerated `packages/3-extensions/sql-orm-client/test/fixtures/generated/contract.d.ts` (after commit `1db94e76f`) has a missing or shape-broken linkage in the `valueObjects` / `FieldOutputTypes` / `roots` machinery. The break could be on either side — the fixture's emitted shape vs the type machinery in `relational-core/src/types.ts` or `sql-orm-client/src/types.ts`.
   - **3 errors (independent, mechanical):** `orm.types.test-d.ts` lines on `db.Post.where(views.like(…))`, `db.Post.where(views.ilike(…))`, and `agg.sum('title')` are all `TS2578: Unused '@ts-expect-error' directive`. Trait gating now permits/forbids correctly, so the suppressions are stale.
3. **Ruled-out:** swapping `helpers.ts` to `origin/main`'s version produced 17 errors (one more than HEAD) — `helpers.ts` is not the cause. Don't re-swap.

## Scope you own

### Part A — the 3 stale `@ts-expect-error` (mechanical)

Delete the 3 stale `@ts-expect-error` directives in `packages/3-extensions/sql-orm-client/test/orm.types.test-d.ts`. Verify by running `pnpm --filter @prisma-next/sql-orm-client run typecheck` and seeing those 3 errors disappear (16 → 13). Commit as a single signed-off commit:

```
test(@prisma-next/sql-orm-client): delete stale @ts-expect-error directives in orm.types

The three suppressions on `db.Post.where(views.like(…))`,
`db.Post.where(views.ilike(…))`, and `agg.sum('title')` are no longer
needed — trait gating now correctly permits/forbids these calls, so
the suppressions surface as TS2578 ("unused @ts-expect-error
directive"). Drop them.

Signed-off-by: Will Madden <madden@prisma.io>
```

### Part B — the 13-error root cause (judgment)

Diagnose and fix in this order:

1. **Diff the two fixture trees.** The integration-side copy
   `test/integration/test/sql-orm-client/fixtures/generated/contract.d.ts` is the version main expects; the package-local copy
   `packages/3-extensions/sql-orm-client/test/fixtures/generated/contract.d.ts` was regenerated by `1db94e76f`. They should be structurally identical if the emit script's `cp` step now copies `.d.ts` correctly. Confirm: are they identical, or does the integration one have linkage the package one lacks (e.g. `valueObjects`, `FieldOutputTypes`, model-root extension shape)?

2. **If the fixtures diverge:** the emit script's `.d.ts` copy is the bug. Fix the emit script (or regenerate via it) so the package-local `.d.ts` matches what the integration one would emit for the same config. This is likely the cleaner of the two routes.

3. **If the fixtures match but typecheck is still red:** then the type machinery in `relational-core/src/types.ts` or `sql-orm-client/src/types.ts` has a `valueObjects` / `roots` / `FieldOutputTypes` linkage that doesn't line up with the current fixture shape. Find the smallest patch that restores the linkage. Probe candidates: `ExtractFieldOutputTypes`, `FieldStorageJsType`, `DefaultModelRow`, `InferRootRow` — names from the investigation hand-off.

4. Verify by running `pnpm --filter @prisma-next/sql-orm-client run typecheck` and seeing 0 errors.

Commit as one or more focused signed-off commits with intent-driven messages (`fix(@prisma-next/sql-orm-client): …` or `fix(test-fixtures/sql-orm-client): …` per the actual fix shape).

### Part C — validate + push

After Parts A and B both green-typecheck `sql-orm-client`, run the full pre-flight gate sequence:

1. `pnpm build` (should still be 66/66 from prior dispatch — re-verify).
2. `pnpm typecheck` (full repo — must be green now).
3. `pnpm fixtures:check`.
4. `pnpm test:packages`.
5. `pnpm lint:deps`.

If all green: `git push --force-with-lease origin tml-2526-facades-must-re-export-everything-users-import-in-their-app`.

If a gate goes red and it's *related to your changes*, fix it and re-run (you have authority within this scope). If a gate goes red and it's *unrelated* (e.g. PGlite connection flake, the known `harness.ts:38` main-side bug), report and proceed to push anyway — flag the failure in your final report.

## Hard rules

- Do NOT touch any file under `projects/` (orchestrator owns).
- Do NOT edit `pnpm-lock.yaml` by hand. If a lockfile change is needed, run `pnpm install`.
- DCO signoff required on every commit.
- Force-push uses `--force-with-lease`, never plain `--force`.
- If your diagnosis in Part B contradicts the investigation hand-off's hypothesis, that's fine — report the contradiction in your final message so the orchestrator learns.
- Heartbeat to `wip/heartbeats/type-fix.txt` every ~5 min or at commit boundaries.

## Out of scope

- The `harness.ts:38` bug on `origin/main` (already known; not ours).
- CodeRabbit findings (separate review-iteration dispatch will follow).
- QA scenario re-runs (separate dispatch).
- PR description updates (orchestrator owns).
- Any other typecheck failures outside the 4 `sql-orm-client` test-d files (none expected, but if found, report and stop).

## Structured report format

```
## Status
GREEN / YELLOW / RED

## Part A — stale @ts-expect-error
- Commit: <sha> — <one-line summary>
- Result: 3 errors cleared / blocked because <reason>

## Part B — 13-error root cause
- Root cause confirmed: <fixture mismatch / type-machinery linkage / other>
- Fix shape: <one paragraph>
- Commit(s): <sha + one-liner each>
- Result: 13 errors cleared / blocked because <reason>
- Contradictions with investigation hand-off (if any): <list or "none">

## Part C — pre-flight + push
- build: green / red (details)
- typecheck (full repo): green / red
- fixtures:check: green / red
- test:packages: green / red
- lint:deps: green / red
- Push: pushed / withheld — <reason>
- New HEAD sha: <sha>

## Surfaced for orchestrator attention
- <anything>
```
